root_cause.text: The WebSocket frame parser failed to validate that the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length was zero, allowing invalid negative payload lengths to be parsed and processed.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `WsFrameBase.java` adds a check `if (payloadLength < 0)` after reading an 8-byte payload length, throwing a `WsIOException` with the message "wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid" if the condition is met.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The WebSocket frame parser failed to validate that the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length was zero, allowing invalid negative payload lengths to be parsed and processed.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `WsFrameBase.java` adds a check `if (payloadLength < 0)` after reading an 8-byte payload length, throwing a `WsIOException` with the message "wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid" if the condition is met.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add an error message for an invalid 64-bit payload MSB.
intent_record.what: Added a new error message property for an invalid payload size.
intent_record.how: Provides the text for an exception thrown when the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload is set.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add boundary check to reject WebSocket frames with negative payload lengths.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to check if payloadLength is negative and throw a WsIOException if it is.
intent_record.how: Prevents the processing of WebSocket frames with invalid payload lengths by throwing a protocol error.

intent_record.intent_summary: Document a WebSocket payload validation fix in the changelog.
intent_record.what: Added an XML changelog entry detailing a fix for bug 64563 related to WebSocket payload length validation.
intent_record.how: Updates project documentation to reflect a recently implemented bug fix.

grouping_result.intent: Document a WebSocket payload validation fix in the changelog.
Add boundary check to reject WebSocket frames with negative payload lengths.

consistency_review.core_intent: Fix and document a WebSocket payload validation issue regarding invalid 64-bit payload lengths.

relevance_review.reason: The group adds an error message specifically for invalid 64-bit payload MSB and documents the fix, directly addressing the root cause of the vulnerability.

The group directly addresses the root cause by adding a boundary check to reject WebSocket frames with negative payload lengths, fixing the failure to validate the most significant bit of the 64-bit payload length.

candidates.rationale: This line parses the 64-bit payload length without validating that the most significant bit is zero, which allows negative payload lengths.
This is the point directly following the payload length parsing, where the validation check should have been inserted to reject negative payload lengths.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement parses the 64-bit payload length from the input buffer without validating that the most significant bit is zero, leading to the processing of invalid negative payload lengths.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Websocket read implementation now uses SocketWrapperBase.read(boolean, ByteBuffer)

commit fc6f2ead8463d06ae73954508927e4f2224c8e77
Author: Violeta Georgieva Georgieva <violetagg@apache.org>
Date:   Mon Sep 26 11:42:26 2016 +0000

    Websocket read implementation now uses SocketWrapperBase.read(boolean, ByteBuffer)
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/tomcat/trunk@1762324 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
index 241a7f962d..84e8132e7d 100644
--
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `processRemainingHeader()` reads an 8-byte payload length when `payloadLength == 127` using `byteArrayToLong(..., 8)`. However, it does not check if the resulting 64-bit signed integer is negative (i.e., verifying the most significant bit is zero) before assigning it to `payloadLength`. Thus, the vulnerability remains.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Don't assume frame starts at the start of the input buffer
